titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Michael Petree Collection of Restau Press.
Petree, Michael Collection of Restau Press
titlestmt.author: Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 01:51

did.unittitle: Michael Petree collection of Restau Press

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection contains manuscripts, correspondence, proofs, and other papers created by, or related to, the literary publisher Restau Press.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organizeed into two series: I. Writings compiled by Michael Petree, 1954-1986. II. Restau Press, 1969-1972.

bioghist.head: Restau Press
bioghist.p: Restau Press was a short-lived literary press operated by J. Mark Nunnelee in the late 1960s and early 1970s. Nunnelee returned from the war in Vietnam and became a bookdealer and publisher for a brief period. His press published work from the eastern Massachusetts literary scene which resulted in a single issue of Restau: A Journal of Passage in 1970, a pamphlet by Stephen Jonas, and poetry broadsides by writers including Jonas, Gerrit Lansing, John Wieners, and Larry Eigner.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Michael Petree collection of Restau Press. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Restau Press records and J. Mark Nunnelee papers (YCAL MSS 1597).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains writings, proofs, and correspondence relating to Restau Press, which published a single issue in 1970. Writings include submissions to Restau Press and poetry by contemporaries. Notable poets in this collection include John Wieners, Janine Pommy-Vega, Gerrit Lansing, and Jackson Mac Low. The Restau Press records included here contain drafts of Restau: A Journal of Passage and a corrected typescript of Spring and Autumn Annals: A Celebration of the Season by Diane di Prima.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Michael Petree collection of Restau Press is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in alphabetical order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings compiled by Michael Petree, including submissions to Restau Press and poetry by contemporaries. Among the materials are an index of poems from "The Floating Bear", a newsletter created by Diane di Prima and Amiri Baraka in the early 1960s. Notable poets include John Weiners, Janine Pommy-Vega, Gerrit Lansing, and Jackson Mac Low.

did.unittitle: [The Floating Bear manuscripts] Bear Mss F.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged chronologically by date of creation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records related to Restau Press, including correspondence, drafts of Restau: A Journal of Passage and a corrected typescript of Spring and Autumn Annals: A Celebration of the Season by Diane di Prima. The correspondence is a mix of personal and business sent to the editor of Restau, J. Mark Nunnelee, and includes letters from Stephen Jonas and Daniel Shaff.

did.unittitle: Spring and Autumn Annals: A Celebration of the Season by Diane di Prima typescript, corrected
Restau: A Journal of Passage No.1
Restau: A Journal of Passage No.1 proofs
